name: AAV9-TRF1 improved healthspan markers in adult and old mice without median telomere lengthening
summary: AAV9-TRF1 is the most direct intervention signal for the telomere protection and capping track.
statement: AAV9-mediated TRF1 expression in adult and old mice improved several physiological aging markers and reduced short-telomere and telomere-associated DNA-damage signals in some tissues without significantly changing median telomere length.
population_or_model: Adult and old wild-type mice treated with AAV9-TRF1
time_horizon: Healthspan follow-up after adult or old-age treatment
quantitative_note: The source reports improved neuromuscular function, glucose tolerance, cognitive function, subcutaneous fat maintenance, and chronic anemia, plus lower abundance of short telomeres and telomere-associated DNA damage in some tissues.
caveats: The evidence is in mice, not humans.
The study supports an animal-stage capping/protection signal, not a human biomarker or clinical stage.
Vector delivery, tissue distribution, durability, immune response, and oncogenic boundaries remain unresolved for human translation.
